page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e4.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor operating at 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 8 vCPUs, which correspond to 8 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average multi-core and memory performance alongside weaker single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU test scores 951.29, placing it in the bottom 10%, while the multi-core CPU test reaches 7603.91, falling into the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests show average throughput, with read-only small block cached memory at 668.76 GB/sec and uncached large block write memory at 33.48 GB/sec. Single-threaded operations like bzip3 decompression and gzip compression score in the poor to weak ranges. However, the instance maintains average performance in static web serving throughput at 8.41 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 9.13 million operations per second.

Due to its shared CPU allocation and older Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 architecture, the ecs.e4.2xlarge presents a clear tradeoff between high memory capacity and raw single-threaded processing power. It offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU pinning or extreme computational throughput. This resource profile makes the instance suitable for memory-optimized workloads such as caching layers, Redis deployments, moderate database operations, and static web serving, while it is less suited for heavy cryptographic tasks, complex LLM inference, or high-performance computing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e4.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with an 8-core Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The instance features 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB per core, but includes no local storage or GPU hardware, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average multi-core performance (stress-ng multi-core score of 7603.91) and average memory bandwidth, but weak single-core processing and database operations. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and shared CPU model, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive, non-compute-heavy workloads. It is best suited for caching systems like Redis, static web serving, and general-purpose memory-optimized tasks that do not require dedicated CPU resources.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual instance with a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio, designed for caching and memory-intensive workloads on a shared CPU architecture.
